reviews.review_msg: I really dislike this prof. He didnt respond to emails & isnt very helpful when you ask questions. His feedback makes no sense cause he uses a list of possible issues and bolds the one that applies to you with no examples or explanation. The fail exam was fine but just made me wish I hadnt gone to lecture cause it wasnt really related

If you skip a class, god bless you. Doesn't use slides or have notes. Amazing lectures but stressful to take notes in. LOTS of reading, 4-5 PhD level novels are assigned. You'll honestly be there for only 2/3 of the class because he's always late, ends early, and takes long breaks. Writing centric but easy-ish marker. Easy to ask questions.

RECORD LECTURES. Don't ask him, just do it. Don't distribute (because then it's illegal). It will help SO MUCH. He may seem unorganized, but his lectures are actually very interesting and it all connects in the end once you have the pieces to connect it :) good luck fams

Lectures were so disorganized and all over the place. He doesn't have any visual aids so good luck trying to stay focused for 3 hours of him just talking. His grading for papers are so high and doesn't give clear criteria for them either. Overall really really disliked this course so good luck to anyone whoever is taking his courses.
His lecture is so confusing and disorganized. I wasn't able to keep up his lecture as well as his high demanding in writing academic essays
Fantastic lecturer, and very engaging. Cares deeply about student success, marks appropriately. Weekly readings. Would take again.

Professor Malik is a great professor in all respects. She deeply cares about her students' success and makes sure that everyone is understanding the lecture content. The readings that she assigned in this class were very very very relevant and I feel like they gave me an excellent kick start for my academic career. Definitely recommend this class!

Prof. Malik is very passionate about teaching. She made a great effort to keep the class engaged for 3 hours. There is a lot of reading but you can get by without doing all of it. She makes sure everyone can pass. However, the material she gave was politically biased as no conservative papers were given, but this is expected given her background.
